steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table or graph. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is B, $6{x}^{3}$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Combine like terms
steps.content: Both terms have the same variable part, $x^3$, so combine only the coefficients.

steps.title: Subtract the coefficients
steps.content: $11x^3-5x^3=(11-5)x^3=6x^3$. The exponent stays $3$ because the terms are being added or subtracted, not multiplied.

steps.title: Match the choice
steps.content: The equivalent expression is $6x^3$, which matches B.
